description: Safety requirements by task type for instrumentation construction

jsa_items: Verify line depressurized and drained before opening connections
Use bleed valve to confirm zero energy
Install root valves first, close before connecting transmitter
Never stand in front of open process connection
Use thread sealant compatible with process fluid
Never use Teflon tape on oxygen service
permits: Hot work permit if welding connections
Line break permit for opening live process connections

ppe: Safety glasses (mandatory — tubing spring-back is common eye injury)
jsa_items: Deburr ALL tubing cuts inside and outside
Blow out tubing with clean dry air/N2 before connecting
Never use copper for instrument air — use 316SS
Pressure test ALL tubing before connecting to instruments
Cap open tubing ends at end of each shift
hazards: Eye hazard from spring-back during bending

jsa_items: Verify tag number matches P&ID and line number
Check flow direction arrow matches process flow
Verify actuator air supply pressure matches nameplate
Stroke valve full range before connecting to process
Verify fail position matches data sheet
hazards: Heavy equipment (hundreds to thousands of pounds)

safety: Use intrinsically safe calibrators in classified areas
Never apply test pressure exceeding instrument rated range
documentation: Apply calibration sticker with date and initials

safety: Notify operations/control room before ANY loop test
Verify DCS/PLC in manual or test mode before driving outputs
Place warning tags on control valves being tested
Confirm safety interlocks bypassed with proper authorization
Two-person communication required — one at field, one at control room
loop_testing.test_order: Verify power → check signal at field device → verify at marshalling panel → verify in DCS/PLC → drive output to verify actuator

requirements: ALL instruments must have appropriate classification rating (XP, IS, or nonincendive)
IS barriers in safe area marshalling cabinets
XP junction boxes: ALL cover bolts installed — no missing bolts
Seal fittings within 18 in of enclosure in Div 1
Only approved tools — no unapproved phones, cameras, radios
Hot work permits for any spark-producing activity

classifications.div_2: Flammable present only under ABNORMAL conditions

safety: Instrument air must be clean, dry, oil-free
Typical supply: 20 psi for instruments, 60-80 psi for valve actuators
Install filter/regulators at each air supply takeoff
Identify pipe contents from labels or P&ID before working near any pipe
Maintain safe distance from uninsulated hot pipes
Never route instrument tubing in direct contact with hot process piping
Report any leaks (hissing, frost, discoloration) immediately
